sourceMovie.title: Jacob's Ladder
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Jacob's Ladder for the same reason it broke through in 1990: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a mystery rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Adrian Lyne keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Mothman Prophecies
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Jacob's Ladder clicked for you, The Mothman Prophecies (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Black Swan

faq.question: What should I watch after Jacob's Ladder?
faq.answer: Try The Mothman Prophecies, Black Swan, Donnie Darko—each shares DNA with Jacob's Ladder while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Jacob's Ladder?
faq.answer: Jacob's Ladder (1990) is best described as Drama, Mystery, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Jacob's Ladder worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
